definition.en: A method or process used to determine how, when, and in what context a political advertisement is delivered to its intended audience, including the use of algorithms, AI systems, or other automated means. Ad-delivery techniques are optimisation techniques used to increase the circulation, reach or visibility of a political advertisement on the basis of the automated processing of personal data.

usage.en: Ad delivery techniques influence the placement, timing, and frequency of advertisements, often using optimisation algorithms or AI to maximise engagement or reach.

definition.en: An event in which eligible participants formally express a choice or decision to elect representatives or decide on public matters, typically through voting.

usage.en: Use this class to describe an election or referendum to which a political advertisement has a clear and substantial link. The title, level, and date of the event must be disclosed in the transparency notice. The level must be indicated as EU, national, regional, or local.

definition.en: A project, program, or organised effort undertaken to achieve specific objectives, often in a political, legislative, or public context.

usage.en: Use this class to describe a legislative or regulatory initiative to which a political advertisement has a clear and substantial link. The title and level of the initiative must be disclosed in the transparency notice. The title may be shortened.

definition.en: An advertisement with a political subject matter, including information about its content, format, language, publication dates,and links to legislative processes or votes.

usage.en: Use this class to represent a political advertisement, including its content, format, language, publication period, and any links to related elections or legislative processes.

definition.en: The set of criteria, characteristics, or parameters used to define the intended audience or recipients of a political advertisement.

usage.en: Targeting techniques determine who will see a political advertisement, for example, by using demographic, geographic, or behavioural data.

definition.en: A generic method or process used to determine how, when, and in what context a political advertisement is delivered to its intended audience.
A record providing information about a political advertisement, its funding, sponsors, targeting, and related entities, to ensure transparency and compliance with legal requirements.

usage.en: Use this class as the root element for transparency disclosures related to a political advertisement.

definition.en: The method or approach used to calculate the amount or allocation of funding.

usage.en: Describe the methodology used to calculate the aggregated amounts and value of other benefits received by the providers of political advertising services, including whether the figures reflect invoiced, budgeted, and/or charged amounts and whether VAT is included. For in-kind benefits, indicate the valuation method used.

definition.en: A Notice for information provision associated with Consent.

usage.en: Provide a URL or reference to the notice informing users about consent for personal data processing in the context of targeting or ad delivery.

definition.en: Indicates whether the origin of the funding is from the EU, from outside the EU.

usage.en: Use a controlled vocabulary with values "EU", "Non-EU", or "EU and non-EU". If possible, align with official EU country groupings.

definition.en: The planned date for the end of publication of the political advertisement.

usage.en: Provide the planned end date for publication or dissemination.

definition.en: The planned date for the start of publication of the political advertisement.

usage.en: Provide the planned start date for publication or dissemination.

definition.en: A URL linking to information provided pursuant to Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (General Data Protection Regulation), including privacy notices, data subject rights, and relevant data protection policies.
Information about the mechanism for addressing previous non-compliance.

usage.en: Provide details of the previous suspension or discontinuation if previousNonComplianceExists is true, describing the nature of the infringement.

definition.en: Information on the mechanism for notifying possibly non-compliant political advertisements, as required by Article 15(1) of Regulation (EU) 2024/900. This may be a URL to an online notification form, an email address, or a description of the notification process.

usage.en: Provide a URL, email address, or other contact method where individuals can notify the publisher or competent authority about possibly non-compliant political advertisements. If the notification mechanism is online, the link should be described as Notify possibly non-compliant political advertisements.

definition.en: The total number of user comments submitted in response to the resource during its dissemination period.

usage.en: State the total number of user comments on the advertisement during the dissemination period.

definition.en: The total number of times users have indicated a positive reaction (such as "like" or equivalent) to the resource during its dissemination period.

usage.en: State the total number of user "likes" or equivalent positive reactions to the advertisement during the dissemination period.

definition.en: The number of times the resource was viewed.

usage.en: State the total number of times the advertisement was displayed to users during the dissemination period. Provide where technically feasible.

definition.en: A description of the political or administrative level if not covered by the controlled vocabulary.

usage.en: If the initiatives level does not fit standard categories.

definition.en: Any other information relevant to the targeting of the advertisement.

usage.en: Include any additional information relevant to the targeting or ad-delivery process not covered by other attributes.

definition.en: The political or administrative level at which the initiative is undertaken.

usage.en: Specify the political or administrative level at which the initiative is being undertaken.

definition.en: The political or administrative level at which the initiative is undertaken.

usage.en: Specify the political or administrative level at which the election is conducted (e.g. EU, national, regional, local)

definition.en: The date after which a resource or record is no longer required to be retained.

usage.en: Indicates the final date by which the transparency notice and any modifications must be retained. The retention period is seven years after the last publication of the related political advertisement. Calculate as: date of last publication + 7 years.

definition.en: The source of the funding (e.g., public, private, mixed).

usage.en: Use a controlled vocabulary with values "Public", "Private", or "Public and private".

definition.en: Indicates the compliance status of the political advertisement, including whether it has a history of previous non-compliance.

usage.en: Record the compliance status of the political advertisement. If the advertisement or an earlier version has been suspended or discontinued due to an infringement, indicate this here.

definition.en: The objectives or goals of the targeting strategy.

usage.en: Describe the objectives of the targeting strategy, including the inclusion and exclusion parameters and the reasons for choosing those parameters.

definition.en: The parameters or criteria used to target the advertisement.

usage.en: List the specific parameters or criteria used to determine the recipients to whom the advertisement is disseminated (e.g. age range, location, interests).

definition.en: The total monetary amount and the value of other benefits, including in-kind, received by providers of services in exchange for a specific resource or activity.

usage.en: Provide the total monetary value, including both invoiced and in-kind benefits, received by all providers of political advertising services for the specific political advertisement.

definition.en: The total monetary amount and the value of other benefits, including in-kind, received by providers of services in exchange for a series of related resources or activities.

usage.en: Include all invoiced, budgeted, or charged amounts and the monetary value of all benefits in kind received or to be received for the entire political advertising campaign.

definition.en: A description of the ad delivery technique(s) used to determine how, when, and in what context the political advertisement is delivered to its intended audience, including any optimisation, algorithmic, or automated methods.

usage.en: Provide a short description of the ad delivery technique(s) used, including any use of algorithms, artificial intelligence, or automated systems to optimise the circulation, reach, or visibility of the advertisement. If multiple techniques are used, list each and describe its role.

definition.en: Indicates whether an artificial intelligence system was used in the targeting or delivery of a resource to an audience, including a description of its role and functioning.

usage.en: Indicate whether artificial intelligence systems were used in the targeting or ad-delivery of the political advertisement. Include a meaningful description of the AI system and its role.

definition.en: Indicates whether targeting techniques are used for this advertisement.

usage.en: Indicate whether targeting techniques were used for this advertisement (true or false).

definition.en: A description of the targeting technique(s) used to select or define the intended audience for the political advertisement, including the logic, mechanisms, and parameters applied.

usage.en: Provide a short description of the targeting technique(s) used, including the inclusion and exclusion parameters, the logic for audience selection, and the reasons for choosing those parameters. If multiple techniques are used, list each and describe its role.

definition.en: Link to an interface allowing the exercise of data protection rights, such as submitting requests for access, rectification, erasure, or other rights under the applicable data protection legal framework.

usage.en: Provide a URL where users can exercise their data protection rights, such as withdrawing consent or requesting data access.

definition.en: A logo to describe / represent an entity.

Something that happens at a particular place and time, organised by one or more agents for a particular purpose, and is of interest to a general audience. This definition excludes natural events such as earthquakes and volcanic eruptions.

usage.en: Use this class as the common superclass for ElectionEvent. It represents an event that happens at a particular place and time and is of interest to a general audience. In the political advertising context, use it to describe elections or referendums to which a political advertisement may be linked.

definition.en: An amount of money available to finance some project or activity.

usage.en: Use this class to represent the financial structure supporting a political advertisement or campaign, including direct and indirect funding sources.

definition.en: The text data type is a combination of a string and a language identifier.

usage.en:
It is useful for names and descriptions that are available in multiple languages. Where this is so, each version of the data should be included and each one associated with the relevant language identifier. RFC 3066 provides a commonly used set of identifiers for natural languages. This is the set recognised by UN/CEFACT and XML Schema.
Languages are represented by two character codes, optionally followed by a locale definition such as "de" meaning German and "de-at" meaning "German as spoken in Austria."

definition.en: The class of literal values,eg. textual strings and integers.
anyURI represents an Internationalized Resource Identifier Reference (IRI). An anyURI value can be absolute or relative,and may have an optional fragment identifier (i.e.,it may be an IRI Reference).
The boolean datatype represents the logical values true and false.

Date represents top-open intervals of exactly one day in length on the timelines of dateTime, beginning on the beginning moment of each day, up to but not including the beginning moment of the next day).  For non-timezoned values, the top-open intervals disjointly cover the non-timezoned timeline, one per day.  For timezoned values, the intervals begin at every minute and therefore overlap.
Decimal represents a subset of the real numbers, which can be represented by decimal numerals.

Integer is ·derived· from decimal by fixing the value of ·fractionDigits· to be 0 and disallowing the trailing decimal point.  This results in the standard mathematical concept of the integer numbers.  The ·value space· of integer is the infinite set {...,-2,-1,0,1,2,...}.  The ·base type· of integer is decimal.
The string datatype represents character strings in XML.
An idea or notion; a unit of thought.
A temporal entity with zero extent or duration
A temporal interval or instant.
A structured reference that identifies an entity.
A self-employed person, company, or organization that has legal rights and obligations.

usage.en: Use this class to represent any legal entity involved in the context of a political advertisement, including the sponsor, the paying entity, or the entity ultimately controlling the sponsor. Required identity fields include business name, legal name, email address, place of establishment, and, where applicable, registration number.

definition.en: A spatial object that in a human-readable way identifies a fixed location.

usage.en: Use this class to represent a structured postal or establishment address associated with a sponsor, paying entity, or controlling entity in a transparency notice. Address information must include at minimum the street, number, city, postal code and country.

definition.en: A individual human being who may be dead or alive,but not imaginary.

usage.en: Use this class to represent a natural person acting as a sponsor, paying entity, or controlling individual in the context of a political advertisement. Required identity fields include given name, family name, email address, and, where made public, postal address.

definition.en: Entity that is able to carry out action.

usage.en: Use this class to represent any natural or legal person acting as a sponsor, paying entity, or controlling entity in the context of political advertising. It is the common superclass of Person and LegalEntity.

definition.en: A document.
The currency in which the Cost needs to be paid and the value of the Cost is expressed.

usage.en: The possible values for this property are described in the controlled vocabulary Currency Authority Table of Publications Office.

definition.en: The currency in which the Cost needs to be paid and the value of the Cost is expressed.

usage.en: The possible values for this property are described in the controlled vocabulary Currency Authority Table of Publications Office.

definition.en: An electronic address through which the Contact Point can be contacted.

usage.en: Provide the contact details of the sponsor.

definition.en: A numeric value indicating the amount of the Cost.

usage.en: State the total monetary amount received or spent for the political advertising campaign, expressed in the specified currency.

definition.en: A numeric value indicating the amount of the Cost.

usage.en: State the total monetary amount received or spent for the political advertising campaign, expressed in the specified currency.

definition.en: A graphic symbol to represent the Public Organization.

usage.en: Provide an image of the political advertisement. The logo should be used to visually identify the political advertisement.

definition.en: The registered address relationship links a Resource with the legally registered Address.

usage.en: Links an Agent to its legally registered address, i.e. the address of its place of establishment. Use for organisations to record their official registered location.

definition.en: The natural or legal person, public authority, agency or other body which, alone or jointly with others, determines the purposes and means of the processing of personal data in the context of a targeted activity or service.

usage.en: Links the targeting record to the data controller responsible for processing personal data for targeting or ad-delivery purposes. The identity and contact details of the controller must be disclosed in the transparency notice.

definition.en: The election related to the event

usage.en: Links the political advertisement to an election event with which it has a clear and substantial link. The title, level, and date of the election must be disclosed in the transparency notice.

definition.en: Information about the financial resources used for the political advertisement or campaign.

usage.en: Links the Transparency Notice to the funding information for the political advertisement and, where applicable, the political advertising campaign. Aggregated amounts, values of in-kind benefits, their public/private and EU/non-EU origin, and the calculation methodology must be provided.

definition.en: The initiative related to the advertisement.

usage.en: Links the political advertisement to a legislative or regulatory initiative with which it has a clear and substantial link. The title and level of the initiative must be disclosed in the transparency notice.

definition.en: The entity that paid for the political advertisement.

usage.en: Links the Transparency Notice to the natural or legal person that provided remuneration in exchange for the political advertisement, if different from the sponsor or the entity ultimately controlling the sponsor.

definition.en: A paid communication intended to influence the outcome of an election or referendum, or to support or oppose a political party or candidate.
A reference to a previous version of the same resource that was suspended or discontinued due to a regulatory or legal infringement.

usage.en: Links to a previous version of the political advertisement that was suspended or discontinued due to a regulatory or legal infringement.

definition.en: The entity that provides financial or other support for the political advertisement.

usage.en: Links the Transparency Notice to the sponsor of the political advertisement. The identity of the sponsor, including name, email, postal address (where made public), and place of establishment must be disclosed.

definition.en: Information about the techniques used for the political advertisement.

usage.en: Links the Transparency Notice to the targeting and ad-delivery information. Must be populated when the political advertisement uses targeting or ad-delivery techniques based on personal data.

definition.en: Indicates that the Agent (subject) is under the decisive influence or control of another Agent (object), such as a legal entity or natural person.

usage.en: Use this relation to specify the entity ultimately controlling the sponsor, payer, or any other relevant Agent in the context of political advertising. Control should be documented where another entity exercises decisive influence on the composition, voting, or decisions of the organs of the controlled Agent, as required by Regulation (EU) 2024/900 and its implementing act.

definition.en: An amount of money to finance the Political Advertisement.
An alternative name for the resource.

usage.en: Provide the business name or any alternative or commonly used name for the legal entity, if different from its legal name.

definition.en: A class of agents for whom the resource is intended or useful.

usage.en: Specify the actual values and parameters used to define the targeted audience (e.g. "age: 23-30; location: post code 1234). Do not use generic terms, provide concrete inclusion/exclusion criteria.

definition.en: Date of creation of the resource.

usage.en: Provide the date the advertisement was created.

definition.en: An account of the resource.

usage.en: Provide a summary or account of the advertisement content, format, and context.

definition.en: The file format, physical medium, or dimensions of the resource.

usage.en: Indicate the machine-readable format of the transparency notice (e.g. JSON, XML).

definition.en: An unambiguous reference to the resource within a given context.

usage.en: Provide a persistent, unique identifier for the transparency notice (e.g., UUID, URI, or registration number).

definition.en: An unambiguous reference to the resource within a given context.

usage.en: Provide a persistent, unique identifier for the political advertisement (e.g., UUID, URI, or registration number).

definition.en: A language of the resource.

usage.en: The language in which the transparency notice is written. Must match the language of the political advertisement.

definition.en: A language of the resource.

usage.en: Provide the language of the political advertisement using a controlled vocabulary. The transparency notice must be written in the same language as the political advertisement.

definition.en: Date on which the resource was changed.

usage.en: Record the date of the most recent update to the transparency notice. The notice must be kept up-to-date during the entire period of publication of the political advertisement.

definition.en: Date on which the resource was changed.

usage.en: Record the date of the most recent update to the political advertisement.

definition.en: A topic of the resource.

usage.en: Provide a topic or keyword describing the subject matter of the political advertisement.

definition.en: The nature or genre of the resource.

usage.en: Provide a concise, descriptive title for the transparency notice.

definition.en: A name given to the resource.

usage.en: Provide the official or commonly used title of the Initiative.

definition.en: The nature or genre of the resource.

usage.en: Provide the official or commonly used title of the political advertisement. If the advertisement is linked to an election or initiative, the title may reference the relevant event or process.

definition.en: A name given to the resource.

usage.en: Provide the official name of the public event. For elections and referendums, the title may be indicated by reference to the category of authorities being elected (e.g. 'parliamentary elections') or to a specific type (e.g. 'constitutional referendum').

definition.en: The nature or genre of the resource.

usage.en: Specify the type of initiative (e.g. legislative, regulatory).

definition.en: The nature or genre of the resource.

usage.en: Specify the format or medium of the political advertisement (e.g. video, audio, display, print, online). This determines which labelling format requirements apply.

definition.en: A classification assigned to an Agent.

usage.en: Indicate whether the agent is a natural person or a legal entity. This determines which identity fields are required.

definition.en: The nature or genre of the Public Event.

usage.en: Specify the type of public event using a controlled vocabulary. Specify if the Public Event is an election of a referendum.

definition.en: Further information about the subject resource.

usage.en: A URL where the transparency notice can be accessed or retrieved. Provide a dereferenceable URL ensuring easy retrieval during the publication period.

definition.en: A string of characters to uniquely identify a concept.

usage.en: Provide the official registration number of the legal entity as assigned by the relevant national or EU authority (e.g., company register, electoral register, or interest representation register). Use the format and value as issued by the authority.

definition.en: The association of a temporal entity (instant or interval) to any thing.

usage.en: Provide the date or date range of the public event. For elections and referendums, this corresponds to the official date(s) of the vote.

definition.en: A secondary identifier of the Dataset

usage.en: Provide any additional identifiers assigned by publishers or platforms (e.g., platform-specific ad ID, publisher registration number). Include metadata about the organisation that published the identifier.

definition.en: The unambiguous reference to the Agent.
The end of the period.

usage.en: Provide the date when the advertisement publication or dissemination ends.

definition.en: The end of the period.

usage.en: Indicate the last date on which the targeted advertisement was disseminated or visible to users.

definition.en: The start of the period.

usage.en: Provide the date when the advertisement publication or dissemination starts.

definition.en: The start of the period.

usage.en: Indicate the first date on which the targeted advertisement was disseminated or visible to users.

definition.en: The name under which the Legal Entity is legally registered.

usage.en: Provide the name under which the legal entity is legally registered. Where the entity operates in more than one official language, provide the legal name in each relevant language where the encoding allows.

definition.en: An "address representation" as conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Address Representation data type. The locn:addressId property may be used to link this locn:Address to other representations.

usage.en: Links an Agent to its postal address. For natural persons, only provide if the address has been made public. For organisations, use as the main correspondence address.

definition.en: A globally unique identifier for each instance of an Address.

usage.en: A globally unique identifier for each instance of an address. Use this property where a persistent, machine-readable identifier for the address is available.

definition.en: The name of the uppermost level of the address, almost always a country.

usage.en: Provide the country of the address using a controlled vocabulary.

definition.en: The uppermost administrative unit for the address, almost always a country. The domain of locn:adminUnitL1 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.

usage.en: Indicate the country where the initiative is taking place, using the official name or code.

definition.en: The uppermost administrative unit for the address, almost always a country. The domain of locn:adminUnitL1 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.

usage.en: Indicate the country where the vote is taking place, using the official name or code.

definition.en: The name of a secondary level/region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities.

usage.en: Provide the city or municipality of the address.

definition.en: The region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities. The domain of locn:adminUnitL2 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.

usage.en: Provide the official name of the subnational administrative unit (e.g., region, province, state) as defined by national or regional standards where the initiative is taking place.

definition.en: The region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities. The domain of locn:adminUnitL2 is locn:Address and the range is a literal, conceptually defined by the INSPIRE Geographical Name data type.

usage.en: Provide the official name of the subnational administrative unit (e.g., region, province, state) as defined by national or regional standards where the initiative is taking place.

definition.en: The complete address written as a string.

usage.en: Provide the complete, human-readable address as a single text string, including all relevant address components (e.g. street, number, postal code, city, country).

definition.en: Proper noun(s) applied to the real world entity identified by the locator. The locator name could be the name of the property or complex, of the building or part of the building, or it could be the name of a room inside a building.

usage.en: The locator name could be the name of the property or complex, of the building or part of the building, or it could be the name of a room inside a building.

definition.en: The code created and maintained for postal purposes to identify a subdivision of addresses and postal delivery points.

usage.en: Post codes are common elements in many countries' postal address systems.

definition.en: A name created and maintained for postal purposes to identify a subdivision of addresses and postal delivery points.

usage.en: Usually a city, for example "Paris".

definition.en: The name of a passage or way through from one location to another.

usage.en: A thoroughfare is usually a street, but it might be a waterway or some other feature. For example, "Avenue des Champs-Élysées".

definition.en: The hereditary surname of a family.

usage.en: Provide the hereditary surname of the natural person.

definition.en: The name(s) that identify the Person within a family with a common surname.

usage.en: Provide the given name(s) of the natural person.

definition.en: The website of the Public Event.

usage.en: Provide the URL of the official website or landing page for the political advertisement.

definition.en: The website of the Public Event.

usage.en: Provide the URL of the official website where information on the modalities for participation in the election or referendum can be found.

definition.en: Indicates association with Personal Data.

usage.en: Specify the categories of personal data processed (e.g. demographic data, browsing behaviour, inferred political affiliation).

definition.en: Indicates policy applicable or used.

usage.en: Provide a link to the internal policy describing how targeting and ad-delivery techniques are used.
